userStories.title: Expand stack to see messages
userStories.description: Click a CardStack to expand it and see individual messages with timestamps in chronological order. Click again or press Escape to collapse.
testingProcedure: Navigate to localhost:3000
Click on a card stack
Verify it expands to show individual messages with timestamps
Click again or press Escape to collapse
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a stack expands it to show messages
Each message shows text and timestamp
Messages are in chronological order
Stack collapses on second click or Escape

userStories.title: Draggable spatial canvas
userStories.description: Replace the static grid layout with a spatial canvas. Stacks render in a default solitaire (column) layout but can be dragged to any position on a finite canvas. Install @dnd-kit/core for drag interaction.
testingProcedure: Navigate to localhost:3000
Verify stacks render in column layout by default
Drag a stack to a new position
Verify it stays where dropped
acceptanceCriteria: Stacks render in solitaire column layout by default
Stacks are draggable to any position on the canvas
Canvas has defined bounds (finite, not infinite scroll)
Dropped stacks stay in their new position

userStories.title: Persist canvas layout per-day
userStories.description: Save canvas positions to localStorage keyed by dayKey. On reload, restore positions. Each day has its own independent layout. Add a 'Reset Layout' button that returns to solitaire default.
testingProcedure: Drag a stack to a new position
Reload the page
Verify the stack is still in the moved position
Navigate to a different day and verify it has its own layout
Click Reset Layout and verify stacks return to solitaire columns
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas positions persist across page reloads
Each day has independent saved layout
Reset Layout button returns to default solitaire columns
Positions stored in localStorage keyed by dayKey

userStories.title: Archive stacks
userStories.description: Add an archive action to each stack (button or context menu). Archived stacks collapse visually and gather in the bottom-right corner of the canvas as a small pile. They remain visible but visually distinct (muted, smaller).
testingProcedure: Click the archive button on a stack
Verify it collapses and moves to bottom-right
Verify it looks visually distinct (muted/smaller)
Verify archived state persists on reload
acceptanceCriteria: Each stack has an archive action
Archived stacks collapse to bottom-right corner
Archived stacks are visually distinct (muted, smaller)
Archive state persists across reloads

userStories.title: Label stacks
userStories.description: Add ability to attach freeform labels to stacks. Labels display as small chips on the card. Labels persist across reloads via localStorage.
testingProcedure: Click to add a label on a stack
Type a label name and confirm
Verify the label appears as a chip on the card
Reload and verify label persists
acceptanceCriteria: Can add one or more freeform labels to a stack
Labels display as chips on the card
Labels persist across reloads
Can remove a label

userStories.title: Merge stacks
userStories.description: Drag one stack onto another to merge them. The merged stack combines messages from both in chronological order. The merge persists across reloads.
testingProcedure: Drag stack A onto stack B
Verify a merge prompt or action appears
Confirm the merge
Verify the resulting stack contains messages from both, chronologically ordered
Reload and verify merge persists
acceptanceCriteria: Can merge two stacks via drag-onto interaction
Merged stack contains all messages chronologically
Merge persists across reloads
Original separate stacks no longer appear

userStories.title: Highlights auto-suggest via OpenRouter
userStories.description: Add a batch script that sends day stacks to OpenRouter LLM to detect 'cool' or viral-worthy moments. Store suggestions in localStorage. Run as part of the nightly workflow or manually.
testingProcedure: Run the highlights batch script manually
Verify it calls OpenRouter with stack content
Verify suggestions are stored locally
Verify suggested highlights appear in the UI with confirm/dismiss buttons
acceptanceCriteria: Batch script sends stacks to OpenRouter for highlight detection
Suggestions stored in localStorage with stack IDs
UI shows suggested highlights with confirm/dismiss
Confirmed highlights persist; dismissed ones are hidden

userStories.title: Highlights & Bangers surface
userStories.description: Dedicated highlights view that shows confirmed highlights across all days. Accessible from the main navigation. Shows the highlight message in context of its stack.
testingProcedure: Confirm a few highlights from suggestions
Navigate to the Highlights & Bangers view
Verify confirmed highlights are displayed
Verify each highlight shows context from its stack
acceptanceCriteria: Dedicated highlights page/section accessible from nav
Shows all confirmed highlights across days
Each highlight shows stack context
Empty state when no highlights confirmed yet

tigers: Canvas interaction complexity — @dnd-kit setup with free positioning + persistence
Topic detection accuracy — time gaps are reliable, topic splits may misfire
OpenRouter dependency — network + cost for local-first tool
elephants: Canvas vs solitaire tension — solitaire is auto-layout, canvas is container, Reset returns to default
Full loop scope — phased delivery keeps each step independently useful
mitigations: Use @dnd-kit with grid snapping before freeform
Time gaps as default, topic detection as refinement, manual merge as escape hatch
Pin to known schema, add version detection
Batch LLM calls during cron only, cache locally
Clear UX: auto-layout is default, manual positioning overrides it
Each user story is shippable independently
